div.p: Invariant: advance() is the only path that mutates current, line, column

g.text: advance() ← sole mutator of cursor + position

div.p: Scanner state AFTER _match('=') → True source = ">=42" start = 0 unchanged current = 2 → now points to 4 line = 1 unchanged column = 3 (advanced past =) _current_lexeme() = ">=" ✓

Core Invariant advance() is the sole and exclusive updater of self.current, self.line, self.column. No code path may do self.current += 1 directly. _match() enforces this by always delegating to advance().
Scanner state BEFORE call source = ">=42" start = 0 → points to > current = 1 → points to = ← next char line = 1 column = 2 (after consuming >)
Consequence: column stays at 2 instead of 3 After 5 such calls → column off by 5 Error messages point to wrong location IDE highlights wrong token

text.tspan: // CORRECT but verbose — _match() wraps this atomically
// CORRECT but verbose — _match() wraps this atomically

div.p: _match() makes this atomic and names the intent: "try to consume exactly this character" Callers cannot forget the advance() call.
